steps.title: Identify the research finding's contradiction with the empirical data
steps.content:
Desai et al. found that democratic strength combined with low inequality restrains inflation.

Yet Austria—which has both democratic strength and low inequality—experienced 2.08% inflation, higher than Cameroon's 0.64%, despite Cameroon having weak institutions and high inequality.

This reversal is the key signal.

steps.title: Apply the signal: what would the research finding suggest?
steps.content:
This contradiction means Austria's high inflation must stem from factors outside its political structure and inequality levels.

Choice A — factors other than Austria's political structure contributed to the country's inflation rate exceeding that of Cameroon in 2017. directly states this: other factors contributed to Austria's elevated inflation.

The alternatives either appeal to measurement error, reverse the causal relationship the data suggests, or discuss a hypothetical scenario that doesn't resolve the current anomaly.

That matches Choice A — factors other than Austria's political structure contributed to the country's inflation rate exceeding that of Cameroon in 2017..
